title: Real Estate Lead Qualification Specialist — Full Time, PH Daytime Hours, $800–$1,200/mo + Bonus
description:
We are a growing real estate & vacation rental digital platform hiring a full-time Lead Qualification Specialist to respond to inbound leads within 15 minutes, qualify them using our script, and hand warm prospects to our sales team through our Kommo CRM. You'll work standard Philippine daytime hours — no night shift.



This is not a cold calling role. You will be working inbound leads that have already raised their hand. Your job is to respond fast, ask smart qualifying questions, and d
 who gets passed to our closers.

We are looking for someone who wants a long-term home — we invest in our people, pay on time, provide performance bonuses, and promote from within as we scale toward 24/7 lead coverage.



What You'll Do



Monitor new leads in Kommo CRM and reply / call within 15 minutes during your shift

Qualify leads using our provided script (timeline, budget, motivation, location)

Log all conversations and qualification notes clearly in Kommo

Book warm leads directly onto our sales team's calendars

Send follow-up messages to unresponsive leads on a defined cadence

Flag high-priority leads to the sales team immediately via our messaging system

Hit weekly KPIs: response time, qualification rate, handoff acceptance



What We Require (please do not apply if you don't meet these)



Minimum 1 year experience in inside sales, SDR, lead qualification, or a similar role

Excellent written and spoken English — you will be messaging and calling worldwide clients directly

CRM experience (Kommo, HubSpot, GoHighLevel, Salesforce, or similar) — Kommo specifically is a big plus

Reliable home internet (minimum 25 Mbps) and backup power solution

Quiet work environment with a good headset for occasional calls

Availability for 9am–6pm Philippine Time (same as Bali/WITA), Monday–Friday, with occasional Saturday coverage

At least 40 hours/week commitment — this is a full-time role, not a side hustle.



Bonus Points For



Prior real estate industry experience (Bali, SE Asia, or international markets especially)

Experience with Kommo CRM specifically

Experience using WhatsApp Business, SMS, and
 for lead communication

Previous role with strict response SLAs.



Compensation



$800–$1,200 USD/month depending on experience (paid bi-monthly via Wise)

Performance bonus tied to qualified-lead conversion rate



How to Apply — READ CAREFULLY

To show us you read this entire post, your application must:



Start with the phrase: "I'm your real estate lead qualifier"

Answer these four questions in the body of your application:



Q1: Describe one time you had to qualify an inbound lead. What questions did you ask and how did you d
 if they were warm?

Q2: What CRMs have you used, and for how long on each?

Q3: What are your internet speed (download/upload) and your backup plan for power outages?

Q4: Can you commit to 9am–6pm Philippine Time, Monday–Friday, with occasional Saturday coverage? Do you currently have any other jobs or commitments during those hours?





Include your resume and a 60-second voice recording (Vocaroo or Google Drive link) answering: "Why should we hire you for this specific role?"



Applications missing any of the above will not be reviewed. We are strict about this because attention to detail is critical in this role.

title: Product Research Specialist (Ecommerce)
description:
Compensation: Paid per task



Let’s be direct.  

This role exists to find products that sell. Not ideas. Not trends for fun. Winners.



If you like guessing, this is not the job. If you like evidence, keep reading.



The Mission  

You identify products with real demand, clear positioning, and pricing that fits a structured store. Your work feeds the entire pipeline. Bad research poisons everything downstream.



What You Will Do  

• Research products for ecommerce stores  

• Validate demand using platform-native signals  

• Identify clear product angles and use cases  

• Analyze pricing logic and margin viability  

• Flag saturation, gimmicks, and weak offers  

• Organize findings cleanly for handoff to the team



Research Standards (Non-Negotiable)  

• Products must fit defined pricing tiers:

  – Low budget  

  – Mid quality  

  – Upscale  

• No random pricing logic  

• No novelty junk  

• No trend chasing without proof  

• Products must support clean branding and ad testing



What “Done” Looks Like  

Each task delivers:  

• 3–5 vetted product candidates  

• Clear reason each product should sell  

• Target price range and positioning tier  

• Notes on audience, pain point, and angle  

• Links and reference sources  

• Clean formatting. No clutter.



Pay Structure  

• Paid per completed research task  

• Consistent work if output is usable  

• Accuracy and judgment matter more than speed



Requirements  

• Proven product research experience  

• Strong pattern recognition  

• Understands ecommerce fundamentals and margins  

• Comfortable saying “this won’t work”  

• Clear written communication  

• Reliable availability



You’ll Do Well Here If  

• You eliminate bad products fast  

• You think in systems, not trends  

• You understand that most products fail  

• You don’t get emotionally attached to ideas



How to Apply  

Send:  

1. A brief summary of your product research experience  

2. One example of a product you would reject and why  

3. Your availability



All products will go through management.

title: Performance Video Editor / Media Buyer
description:
Compensation: Paid per task



Let’s get aligned fast.  

This role exists to make ads convert. Not to impress. Not to experiment. To sell.



If you are optimizing for aesthetics, this is not the job. If you are optimizing for response, keep reading.



The Mission  

You edit short-form ads designed to stop the scroll and trigger action. Every cut has a purpose. Every second earns its place.



Platforms: TikTok, Instagram Reels,
.



What You Will Do  

• Edit short-form paid ads (8–12 seconds)  

• Build a strong hook in the first 2 seconds or the ad dies  

• Control pacing, cuts, captions, and rhythm for retention  

• Follow brand references exactly. No interpretation.  

• Produce multiple variations per task for testing



What “Done” Looks Like  

Each task requires:  

• 3–6 ad variations  

• Clean exports, correctly named  

• Hook-driven structure:

  – Pattern interrupt  

  – Immediate product clarity  

  – Emotional trigger  

  – Clear call to action  

• Delivery within the agreed deadline. No drift.



Pay Structure  

• Task-based pay  

• Typical weekly range: $40–$50  

• Volume and performance determine upside



Requirements  

• Proven experience editing paid ads (portfolio required)  

• Understands conversion, not just visuals  

• Proficient in CapCut or Premiere Pro  

• Can follow references with precision  

• Fast, reliable, detail-focused



You’ll Win Here If  

• You think in hooks, not transitions  

• You can kill your favorite edit if it hurts performance  

• You deliver clean work without reminders  

• You understand that ads are systems, not art projects



How to Apply  

Send:  

1. Your paid ads portfolio (not cinematic edits)  

2. The platform you edit on  

3. Your typical turnaround time per task



This role is twice as important because it handles 2 things that come together for the most important piece: conversion.

title: eLearning Developer
description:
Coast Wide Training Solutions is a Vocational Education and Compliance Consulting service focused on supporting the regulatory compliance, quality development, and improvement of Registered Training Organisations across Australia.

We work with RTOs to provide regulatory compliance advice, guidance, and development support, specifically catering our consulting services to improve RTO operations. We support our clients by providing:

• Regulatory compliance and RTO administrative advice, guidance, and development support

• Robust Quality Management Systems aligned to the Standards for RTOs 2025

• Internal review and monitoring activities and rectification work

• Superior quality training, learning, and assessment resources - contextualised, compliant, and student-focused



When providing our services, Coast Wide Training Solutions upholds our core values of:

• Excellence: achieving high quality and superior outcomes for our clients

• Integrity: doing the right thing, even when no-one is watching

• Teamwork: working collaboratively with each other and our clients

• Communication: listening, understanding, and building trust to maintain positive relationships



The Role…

We are seeking a skilled eLearning developer with hands-on experience in Articulate Rise 360 and Storyline 360. Someone who is confident working independently, following a detailed brief, and delivering polished, on-brand digital learning content to a professional standard - on time, and without significant hand-holding.



CWTS is building out its RTO Administration Coaching and RTO Compliance Coaching course series which includes 23 online courses aligned to the Standards for RTOs 2025. Course content is already written and structured. Your role is to convert that content into engaging, professionally built Rise 360 courses with embedded Storyline 360 interactions, using our templates, brand guidelines, and production specifications.



This role commences as a freelance contract engagement on a per-course flat rate basis, working remotely. The first course is a paid trial with rate increases available for consistently high-quality work. 



You Will Be Responsible For…

• Converting structured course content into polished Articulate Rise 360 courses using the CWTS course template

• Building and customising Articulate Storyline 360 interactive blocks and embedding them into Rise 360 as content requires, including hotspots, branching scenarios, and knowledge checks

• Creating supporting graphics in Canva to the CWTS brand specification and saving all assets to the correct CWTS project folders

• Following the CWTS production brief and course scope document for each course, accurately and completely

• Completing the CWTS quality assurance checklist before submitting each course for review

• Packaging and uploading the completed SCORM file to the designated location

• Recording time worked against each course task in our project management platform (basic training provided)

• Communicating proactively - flagging questions or issues before they become problems



You Will Have…



Essential Skills and Experience

• Confident, production-level experience in Articulate Rise 360

• Proven ability to build and customise interactive content in Articulate Storyline 360, including hotspots, branching scenarios, tabs, and knowledge check activities

• Proficiency in Canva, creating branded graphics to a supplied style guide

• Experience publishing Rise 360 courses as SCORM packages

• Strong attention to brand compliance, ability to follow a detailed brief without deviation

• Strong written English, you will lightly edit and reformat content to suit the eLearning medium

• A portfolio of completed Rise 360 work (with at least one example including embedded Storyline 360 interactions)



Remote Work Skills 

Due to the nature of this role, the following are non-negotiable:

• Time management and the ability to meet agreed deadlines

• Self-directed and organised - capable of working with limited supervision

• Responsive communication - prompt replies, clear updates, professional tone

• Adaptable and accountable - ability to take feedback and act on it without defensiveness



Highly Desirable

• Basic project management skills using online platforms

• Experience developing eLearning content for the Australian VET or RTO sector

• Familiarity with compliance-focused or procedural learning content

• Understanding of adult learning principles

• Experience working with Australian clients or to WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility standards



Equipment Requirements

You are expected to have and maintain the following as part of your home office setup:

• Reliable computer (Windows or Mac) capable of running Articulate 360

• Stable internet connection 

• Webcam and microphone suitable for video interviews and tea
etings

• Quiet, dedicated workspace

• Computer protected by up-to-date antivirus software and a secure Wi-Fi connection (required for handling CWTS course materials)



Articulate 360 software licence will be provided by CWTS. All other equipment and software is the contractor's responsibility.?





TO APPLY:

Your application must include:

• Resume - current, relevant experience highlighted

• Portfolio - minimum 2 examples of completed Rise 360 courses. At least one must include embedded Storyline 360 interactions. Links or SCORM files accepted.

• Canva samples - 2–3 examples of eLearning graphics or video assets you have created in Canva

• Cover note - no more than 200 words. Tell us: your Articulate 360 experience in years, your current daily availability, and one thing about your work that you are most proud of.



Applications without a portfolio will not be considered.



We are looking for the right person. If that is you, we would love to hear from you.
